content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory model layer decomposition and cross-cutting concerns; The v3 data model is intentionally split into layered components—Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate context), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit)—to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantics, lifecycle/retention, and repairability. It also defines two cross-cutting concerns: scope and retrieval lifecycle.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model section defining v3 architecture in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; Entity serves as an anchor for claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.; scope=Applies to Entity purpose in this document section.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_constraint; MemoryRun run logs must not disappear silently.; scope=Invariant for MemoryRun in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.9
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support typed memory.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Entity component as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge; In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; alias and merge mechanics are constrained so merge history is preserved, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges are reversible via audit trail.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> used_for; MemoryRun is used for audit trail and debugging of memory pipeline execution details.; scope=Applies to the MemoryRun component definition in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.9
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> has_constraint; MemoryRun should include input_hash for idempotency and retry safety.; scope=Invariant guidance for MemoryRun in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.9
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support lifecycle and retention.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support lineage.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support reconcilable updates.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support repairability.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support richer semantic context.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; The v3 data model should support temporal truth.; scope=Applies to the v3 data model design described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Scope/truth-retrieval separation plus explicit retention policy and lifecycle metadata in v3; For most memory items except raw Source, v3 uses machine-readable scope_json (alongside human-readable scope) to bound applicability for retrieval and reconciliation; truth status is separate from retrieval_state. v3 also requires explicit retention policy: low-use notes may move to cold, old resolved items to archived, sensitive source data supports soft/hard delete on request, and historical claims may remain explainable while being deprioritized in top retrieval. Recommended retention metadata includes retention_class, expires_at, deleted_at, and archived_at.; scope=Applies to cross-cutting field definitions and retention guidance in 02. Data Model (sections incl. retention/forgetting) of agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_constraint; In v3 retention policy, sensitive source data must be soft- or hard-deleted upon request.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 6
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_constraint; Entity is the canonical memory node.; scope=Applies to Entity definition in this document section.
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; `Note` is an intermediate semantic fragment for retrieval-ready but non-final knowledge; The `Note` component is positioned between raw source and final claims: it stores important contextual knowledge (e.g., decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) that is useful for retrieval without hardening into immutable fact, with invariants enforcing one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=v3; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=v3; reason=In this data-model context, 'v3' refers to the existing concept 'v3 memory data model'.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=02. Data Model|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=02. Data Model agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке MARKDOWN Document title: 02. Data Model Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке Document lines: 619-628 ## 8. Модель данных в одной строке - `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста - `Task` = что должно быть сделано - `Episode` = чему система научилась - `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26] EXTERNAL

Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Модель данных в одной строке in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md"},"summary":"Section 8 provides a compact glossary of component roles in the v3 memory model: Source captures observed input, Entity identifies the object, Note stores important-but-not-yet-hardened context, Claim stores normalized facts, Profile is stable-context projection, Task is pending work, Episode is learned outcome, and MemoryRun records how memory was updated.","tags":["data_model","component_roles","glossary","doc_section"],"title":"One-line role map of v3 memory components","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null}]}
